ogTitle: Tamborine Mountain vs Tamrookum: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Tamborine Mountain and Tamrookum.
heading: Tamborine Mountain vs Tamrookum — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Tamborine Mountain, Queensland and Tamrookum, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Tamrookum has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Tamborine Mountain (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Tamborine Mountain or Tamrookum safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Tamborine Mountain has a flood risk score of 46/100, while Tamrookum scores 35/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Tamborine Mountain scores 42/100 for bushfire risk. Tamrookum scores 30/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Tamborine Mountain or Tamrookum?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Tamrookum has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.92% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
